S.J.Res. 198 | A joint resolution providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services of the Department of Health and Human Services relating to "Medicare Program; Implementation of Prior Authorization for Select Services for the Wasteful and Inappropriate Services Reduction (WISeR) Model". | Sen. Ron Wyden (D-OR) | Finance Committee

📅 16-07-2026 | 🗳️ Motion to proceed rejected (46-50)
What's next: The bill stays on the calendar (a new motion can be made)

Summary: This joint resolution would reject a Medicare rule that requires doctors to get advance approval from insurance companies before providing certain medical services under a pilot program aimed at reducing unnecessary care. If passed, it would overturn the rule and prevent Medicare from implementing this prior authorization requirement.

text:
S. 629 | Emergency Conservation Program Improvement Act of 2025 | Sen. Deb Fischer (R-NE) | Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry Committee

📅 12-07-2026 | 📜✍️ Became Public Law
What's next: Takes effect (on enactment unless the text sets other dates)

Summary: The law expands two federal disaster-assistance programs for farmers and forest owners by allowing them to receive advance payments covering up to 75% of emergency repair and restoration costs, rather than the current 25% limit, and extends the deadline to use those funds from 60 days to 180 days. It also broadens eligibility to include damages from wildfires caused by human activity or the federal government, and makes advance payments available for the first time under the forest restoration program.
[AI-Generated]

S. 4784 | National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027 | Sen. Roger Wicker (R-MS) | Armed Services Committee

📅 20-07-2026 | 🗳️ Motion to proceed filed
What's next: The Senate votes on taking it up (the motion can be withdrawn)

Summary: The bill would authorize spending for the Department of Defense's military operations, construction projects, and related activities for fiscal year 2027, while also setting the number of military personnel allowed during that period.
